releases.body:
## What's Changed in v2.8.1

### 🚀 New Features

- Add Vylen Linux to distribution packages and fix badge formatting - by @CelestifyX (#2405)
- Add Claude automation hooks for linting and PII protection - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2424)
- Add trial of simili-bot for duplicate issue detection - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2438)
- Add workflow to backfill simili-bot Qdrant index - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2440)
- Add MQTT implementation plan for incoming call events - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2445) ([#2370](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2370))
- Add Wayland GPU auto-disable troubleshooting guide - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2461) ([#2410](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2410), [#2459](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2459))

### 🐛 Bug Fixes

- Fix: Resolve high-severity lodash-es and serialize-javascript vulnerabilities in docs-site - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2402)
- Fix call status detection when hanging up from popup window - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2406) ([#2358](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2358))
- Update dependencies, including Electron, and resolve follow-redirects CVE - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2420)
- Fix: Unbreak cross-distro smoke test by correcting AppImage download and checks - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2422)
- Update DOMPurify to 3.4.0 for security and bug fixes (#2427)
- Fix: Pin Docusaurus v4 future flags for docs-site build compatibility - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2429)
- Fix: Prevent camera resolution override from blurring screen shares - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2437) ([#2432](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2432))
- Fix: Preserve Teams shell when joining meetings and add return path - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2439) ([#2322](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2322))
- Fix simili-bot seeder workflow to download the release binary directly - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2441)
- Fix simili-bot seeder flags for v0.2.0-rc.1 - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2442)
- Fix: IPC security wrapper now correctly removes listeners - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2446) ([#2436](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2436))
- Fix: Prevent duplicate simili-bot comments on issues and PRs - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2448)
- Fix: Resolve AppImage URL for cross-distro smoke tests - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2449)
- Fix: Update @xmldom/xmldom to 0.8.13 for security improvements (#2458)
- Fix: Prevent mid-call reloads by narrowing auth recovery trigger - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2460) ([#2428](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2428))

### 📚 Documentation

- Update Docusaurus and React dependencies in docs site (#2419)
- Update TypeScript to 6.0.3 in docs-site (#2444)

### 🔧 Maintenance

- Update electron, playwright, and eslint to latest minor/patch versions (#2400)
- Update Electron and globals dependencies (#2418)
- Propose ADR-020 multi-account profile switcher - by @jpenberthy (#2426)
- Wire up renderer error forwarding and remove login handler leak - by @jpenberthy (#2435)
- Update Electron and ESLint to their latest minor/patch versions (#2443)
- Update roadmap with session outcomes and next steps - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2466)
- Resume MQTT extended status for camera and microphone topics - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2467) ([#2465](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2465))
- Backfill missing changelog entries for five recently merged PRs - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2468)

---

### 📖 Related Documentation

- [Electron Releases](https://releases.electronjs.org/)
- Configuration changes in this release:
  - [MQTT Integration](https://ismaelmartinez.github.io/teams-for-linux/mqtt-integration)
  - [Application Core](https://ismaelmartinez.github.io/teams-for-linux/configuration#application-core)
  - [Authentication / SSO](https://ismaelmartinez.github.io/teams-for-linux/configuration#authentication-sso)
  - [Wayland](https://ismaelmartinez.github.io/teams-for-linux/configuration#wayland)

---

**Quick Links:**

- [Configuration Reference](https://ismaelmartinez.github.io/teams-for-linux/configuration) - All options with defaults
- [Troubleshooting](https://ismaelmartinez.github.io/teams-for-linux/troubleshooting) - Common issues and solutions
- [Installation Guide](https://ismaelmartinez.github.io/teams-for-linux/installation) - Setup instructions

## New Contributors

* @CelestifyX made their first contribution in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/pull/2405
* @jpenberthy made their first contribution in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/pull/2426

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/compare/v2.8.0...v2.8.1

## What's Changed in v2.8.0

### 🚀 New Features

- Add Repo Butler consumer guide for AI agents and health checks (#2387)
- Update Electron to 41.1.0, adding nativeTheme and notification urgency features. (#2395)

### 🐛 Bug Fixes

- Fix: Preserve login identity after session expiry and system resume - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2376) ([#2364](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2364))
- Update @xmldom/xmldom to 0.8.12, fixing whitespace and security issues. (#2384)
- Fix: Pin Node.js version in cross-distro Dockerfiles to resolve test failures - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2386)
- Update setup-node action to support devEngines and fix npm audit issues. (#2389)
- Update lodash to 4.18.1, fixing template and fromPairs ReferenceError (#2394)
- Fix: Update brace-expansion to resolve CVE for process hang and memory exhaustion. - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2397)

### 📚 Documentation

- Update TypeScript to 6.0.2 in docs-site (#2381)

### 🔧 Maintenance

- Remove stale roadmap sections, focus on future content - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2379)
- Update minor dependencies: dbus-native, mqtt, and electron/fuses (#2380)
- Update actions/deploy-pages to v5.0.0, including Node.js 24.x support. (#2388)
- Update roadmap with v2.8.0 progress, cross-distro testing, and Claude Code automations. - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2391)
- Update upstream dependencies. - by @bkohler (#2385)

---

### 📖 Related Documentation

- [Electron v41.1.0 Release Notes](https://releases.electronjs.org/release/v41.1.0)
  - See [Electron 41.x blog post](https://www.electronjs.org/blog/electron-41-0) for major features
- Configuration changes in this release:
  - [Mqtt Integration](https://ismaelmartinez.github.io/teams-for-linux/configuration#mqtt-integration)

- [Troubleshooting](https://ismaelmartinez.github.io/teams-for-linux/troubleshooting) - Common issues and solutions
- [Installation Guide](https://ismaelmartinez.github.io/teams-for-linux/installation) - Setup instructions

openIssues.title: [Bug]: Institutional authentication broken after snap update to rev 1783 (v2.8.1)
openIssues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug on the Microsoft Teams web app (https://teams.microsoft.com)?

No

### Describe the bug

After an automatic snap update of teams-for-linux to revision 1783 (v2.8.1), authentication is broken. A red banner prompts the user to click "Sign in", but clicking the button has no effect. The app enters an infinite AuthFailed loop with a rate guard throttle kicking in after repeated failures.

### What package are you using?

snap

### Version

2.8.1

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. Install teams-for-linux via snap (Ubuntu 24.04)
2. Allow automatic snap update to revision 1783 (v2.8.1), or run sudo snap refresh teams-for-linux
3. Launch Teams
4. Observe the red "Sign in" banner
5. Click "Sign in" — no OAuth window opens, no response
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
Clicking "Sign in" should open an OAuth authentication window (either embedded or via system browser), or the authentication screen should appear on app startup.
```

### Debug

```markdown
Claude found this interesting from the log file attached: 

Failed to call method: org.freedesktop.Secret.Service.ReadAlias
An AppArmor policy prevents this sender from sending this message to this recipient
label="snap.teams-for-linux.teams-for-linux (enforce)"
```

### Additional context

v2.8.0 worked until yesterday and reverting to 2.8.0 fixed the issue for me

[teams_debug.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27159936/teams_debug.log)

labels.name: awaiting user feedback
labels.description: Waiting for user to confirm they still need this feature

openIssues.body:
### Describe the feature

Add support for custom stickers within the application. The idea is to allow users to maintain a personal collection of stickers (images such as PNG, JPG, or GIF) accessible from within the UI.

### Motivation

Microsoft Teams does not support custom user-defined stickers, which limits personalization and expressiveness in conversations.

Since this project already enhances the Teams experience, adding custom sticker support would provide a unique and highly valuable feature not available in the official client. It would improve user engagement and make communication more fun and flexible.

### Alternatives

Currently, the only workaround is manually uploading images as attachments each time, which is slow and inconvenient.

Another alternative would be using external clipboard managers or scripts, but these solutions are not integrated into the Teams workflow and provide a poor user experience compared to a native implementation.

### Additional context

The feature could provide a dedicated panel or section where users can browse their sticker library and quickly send them in chats. When a sticker is selected, it would be automatically sent as an image attachment to the current conversation.

Optionally, this could include:

- A configurable local folder for storing stickers
- Drag & drop support to add new stickers
- Categorization or tagging for easier navigation
- Recently used stickers section

openIssues.title: [Feat]: Add "Don't show again" dismissal for startup config warning dialog

labels.name: ready
labels.description: There is a fix in develop branch (and snap edge). Soon to be released

openIssues.body:
### Describe the feature

Add a persistent per-warning dismissal mechanism to the startup config warning dialog in `app/index.js:441-449`. The dialog reads `config.warnings` (populated by `checkUsedDeprecatedValues()` plus the mutex check added in #2450) and currently pops on every launch while any warning is present, with no way to acknowledge.

Proposed shape:

- Electron's `dialog.showMessageBox({ checkboxLabel: "Don't show this again", ... })` for the UI (native, no custom widget needed).
- When checked, hash the warning string (content-based) and append the hash to `settingsStore` under `warnings.acknowledged = [...]`.
- On subsequent startups, filter `config.warnings` by acknowledged hashes before the dialog check.
- Content-based hashing means any reworded or new warning automatically re-surfaces — users don't silently dismiss something they never saw.

### Motivation

- Some warnings nag a config-fix that users don't want to make. Example: the multi-account / Intune mutex warning in #2450 — users who legitimately have both `multiAccount.enabled` and `auth.intune.enabled` set across different machines, and deliberately prefer Intune on this machine, currently see the dialog every launch with no way to acknowledge.
- For deprecated-config warnings, power users who've read and understand the deprecation may prefer to migrate on their own timeline without the per-launch nag.

### Alternatives

- **Status quo** — keep the dialog firing every launch. Works as a nag for migratable warnings but produces per-launch noise for warnings that describe a user preference rather than a migratable mistake.
- **Don't add to `config.warnings`** — surface only via `console.warn`. Loses visibility for users who don't read logs.
- **Global "never show any config warning" toggle** — too blunt; users lose visibility into future real warnings.
- **Per-warning config keys** (e.g., `multiAccount.suppressMutexWarning`) — fragments the config surface as more warnings land; dismissal shouldn't need a new config schema entry per warning.

### Additional context

- Existing consumers of `config.warnings`: the dialog at `app/index.js:441` and nothing else as of today, so filtering at the dialog-show site is safe.
- Surfaced during review of #2450. No urgency — the multi-account path can stay behind the nag dialog until this lands, then migrate.

openIssues.title: [Feat]: Option to override getUserMedia audio constraints for high-fidelity mic input

labels.name: awaiting user feedback
labels.description: Waiting for user to confirm they still need this feature

openIssues.body:
### Describe the feature

Add a configuration option that lets the wrapper override the `getUserMedia` audio constraints the Teams web client requests, so users can disable `noiseSuppression`, `echoCancellation`, and/or `autoGainControl` at the Chromium/WebRTC layer. This would be the Linux equivalent of the "High fidelity music mode" that Microsoft exposes only on the Windows desktop Teams client.

### Motivation

Users running an external mixer or soundboard into Teams (e.g., RØDECaster Duo, Stream Deck sound pads) currently have sound effects and voice effects filtered or gated out by Chromium's WebRTC audio processing.

- Teams' web client calls `getUserMedia({ audio: { noiseSuppression: true, echoCancellation: true, autoGainControl: true } })`, and Chromium enforces those constraints regardless of Electron CLI feature flags (`WebRtcHybridAgc`, `WebRtcApmInAudioService`, `WebRtcAllowInputVolumeAdjustment`, etc.) — the flags only influence *how* APM runs, not whether it runs.
- Microsoft has not exposed Music mode (or equivalent) in the web/Linux Teams client. The in-app "Noise suppression: Off" toggle controls only Teams' ML layer, not WebRTC APM.
- The existing `media.microphone.disableAutogain` option plus the Chromium feature flags above get basic sound effects (drum stings, EQ'd voice like Megaphone) through, but vocoder/pitch-shift voice effects (e.g., RØDECaster "Robot," "Voice Disguise") still get gated because WebRTC's speech-vs-noise classifier reads them as non-speech and suppresses them.

A wrapper-level constraint override would make Teams usable for podcasters, streamers, and anyone routing hardware audio effects into meetings, without needing to fall back to other clients.

### Alternatives

1. Wait for Microsoft to expose Music mode in the web client — out of this project's control; long-standing gap.
2. Pure Chromium/Electron feature flags — insufficient (see above).
3. PipeWire virtual-source tricks — speculative; WebRTC APM sits downstream of the OS audio layer.

### Additional context

Possible implementation: a renderer-side preload script that monkey-patches `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia` to strip or override audio constraints before delegating to the original implementation, gated on a new config key (e.g. `media.microphone.overrideConstraints` or similar).

Tested environment where effects are partially passing through with existing options:

```json
{
  "media": { "microphone": { "disableAutogain": true } },
  "electronCLIFlags": [
    ["disable-features", "WebRtcAllowInputVolumeAdjustment,WebRtcHybridAgc,WebRtcApmInAudioService"],
    ["enable-features", "WebRtcAllowInputGainControl"]
  ]
}
```

- `teams-for-linux` latest stable (~2026-04)
- CachyOS / Hyprland / PipeWire
- RØDECaster Duo over USB, Duo-side per-channel Processing disabled

Happy to help test if this gets picked up.

labels.name: ready
labels.description: There is a fix in develop branch (and snap edge). Soon to be released

openIssues.body:
Currently the release process uses a custom `prepare-release.yml` workflow with `.changelog/pr-XXX.txt` staging files and a bespoke `generateReleaseNotes.mjs` script. This works but involves several manual steps that release-please would automate.

release-please (Google) maintains an auto-updating Release PR derived from conventional commits. Since the project already enforces conventional-commit discipline, contributors would need zero behaviour change. The maintainer retains full control over timing by choosing when to merge the Release PR.

### What release-please replaces

- Manual version bumping in `package.json` / `package-lock.json`
- The `.changelog/pr-XXX.txt` staging approach and AI-generated changelog bot
- The `scripts/generateReleaseNotes.mjs` categorisation script
- The `prepare-release.yml` workflow

### What needs custom handling

- `appdata.xml` updates: release-please has no built-in awareness of this file. A supplementary Action step is needed to patch it with the release version and changelog content.
- Snap channel promotion workflow remains manual (candidate → stable).

### Suggested approach

1. Set up the release-please GitHub Action for Node.js release type
2. Build a supplementary step for `appdata.xml` patching
3. Test on a few patch releases before relying on it for a minor/major
4. Retire `.changelog/` bot, `prepare-release.yml`, and `generateReleaseNotes.mjs` after confirming it works

### References

- [Project management tools research](https://ismaelmartinez.github.io/teams-for-linux/development/research/project-management-tools-research) — full evaluation and recommendation
- [Current release process](https://ismaelmartinez.github.io/teams-for-linux/development/manual-release-process) — what gets replaced
- [ADR-005: AI-powered changelog generation](https://ismaelmartinez.github.io/teams-for-linux/development/adr/005-ai-powered-changelog-generation) — context on the current changelog approach

openIssues.body:
## Description

After the notification lifecycle fix in PR #2329 (merged), notifications now work consistently. However, the first notification received after app launch shows a title count prefix (e.g. "(1) Name") from the currently active conversation instead of the actual message content from the sender.

Subsequent notifications display correctly.

## Follow-up from

This is a follow-up from #2248. The main issue (subsequent notifications stop firing entirely) was fixed by #2329. This tracks the remaining first-notification quirk reported by @SoldierRMB.

## Screenshot

![First notification quirk](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/16b2c5f1-c255-4c03-90ac-4d44fac6e77a)

## Steps to reproduce

1. Launch the app and log in
2. Have a conversation active in the Teams window
3. Receive a message from a different contact
4. The first notification shows the active conversation's title with a count prefix instead of the incoming message content

## Expected behaviour

The first notification should show the sender name and message content from the incoming message, not the active conversation title.

releases.name: v0.1.0 — Repository Strategist
releases.body:
First release of the Repository Strategist — a GitHub App that maintains institutional memory for software projects and uses it to provide doc-grounded triage and strategic intelligence.

## Features

- Multi-phase triage pipeline: deterministic template parsing (Phase 1), vector search + Gemini suggestions (Phase 2), enhancement context briefs (Phase 4a)
- Synthesis engine: weekly cluster detection, ADR drift detection, upstream impact analysis, posted as shadow repo briefings
- Enhancement research agent: mirror issues in shadow repos, context briefs, full research synthesis with safety gates
- MCP server: four tools exposing triage, synthesis, health, and trend data to Claude Code agents
- Event journal: records all repo activity for temporal analysis
- Auto-ingest: re-embeds changed documentation on push
- Per-repo config via `.github/butler.json`
- Live dashboard at `/dashboard`
- Two-layer safety: structural validator + LLM reviewer on all outputs
- Shadow repo approval gates: `lgtm`/`reject` for triage, `research`/`use as context`/`reject` for enhancements

## Infrastructure

Go 1.26.1 on Google Cloud Run, Neon PostgreSQL with pgvector, Gemini 2.5 Flash, GitHub Actions CI/CD with Workload Identity Federation.

## Documentation

- 13 Architecture Decision Records in `docs/adr/`
- Roadmap with six streams in `docs/plans/2026-03-04-roadmap.md`
- Security policy in `SECURITY.md`

openIssues.title: Simili-bot trial — teams-for-linux (2026-04-20 → 2026-05-20)
openIssues.body:
Weekly-review log for the simili-bot duplicate-detection trial on `IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux`. Full plan, success criteria, and outcome-decision matrix: `docs/plans/2026-04-16-simili-bot-trial.md`.

## Setup

- Installed via `IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux` PR #2438 (workflow) + #2440/#2441/#2442 (seeder iterations)
- Mode: `similarity-only` — related-issue suggestions only, no labels, no auto-close
- Qdrant collection `teams-for-linux-issues` seeded with 1,408 issues on 2026-04-20T10:11Z
- Embeddings: `gemini-embedding-001` @ 3072 dimensions (separate from the triage bot's 768-dim Neon/pgvector index by design — no shared state)
- Trigger: `issues.opened|edited|reopened|labeled` and `issue_comment.created`

## Success criteria (from the plan)

- Precision: >60% of simili-bot suggestions are genuine duplicates/relatives
- Coverage: >=50% of duplicates the maintainer would have flagged manually are surfaced
- Noise: <20% of new issues receive an unhelpful simili-bot comment
- Cost: combined triage + simili-bot usage stays inside the Gemini free tier
- No regression in triage bot's Phase 1 fill rate or dashboard metrics

## Weekly review log

Skim [simili-bot's recent comments on teams-for-linux](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues?q=commenter%3Asimili-bot+sort%3Acreated-desc) once per week, classify each comment as useful / neutral / noise, and note anything surprising.

- [ ] Week 1 review — due 2026-04-27
- [ ] Week 2 review — due 2026-05-04
- [ ] Week 3 review — due 2026-05-11
- [ ] Week 4 review — due 2026-05-18
- [ ] Close-out + `docs/decisions/007-simili-bot-trial.md` — 2026-05-20

## Dashboards to watch

- Triage bot LLM-usage panel: https://triage-bot-lhuutxzbnq-uc.a.run.app/dashboard (cost signal, Phase 1 fill-rate regression)
- Qdrant Cloud dashboard — collection size, query latency
- [simili-bot workflow runs](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/actions/workflows/simili.yml) — failure rate

## Notes / observations

(Add each week's observations as a comment on this issue. Keep it short — useful/neutral/noise counts and any user confusion between the two bots.)

releases.name: v0.3.1
releases.body:
# @ismaelmartinez/generator-atlassian-compass-event-catalog

## 0.3.0

### Minor Changes

-   feat: presentation layer improvements
    -   Populate `service.attachments` from Compass links via structured links (url, title, type, icon)
    -   Categorise links into subsections: Compass, Development, Operations, Documentation, Other
    -   Expose `StructuredLink` type and pass it as optional third parameter to custom markdown templates
    -   Surface `customFields` as a markdown table between Links and Dependencies sections
    -   Map Compass `typeId` to `service.styles.icon` using Lucide icon names

## 0.2.1

### Patch Changes

-   53a5fd2: fix: address security vulnerabilities in token logging, path traversal, and XSS
    -   Prevent potential API token leakage via error logs in team fetch failure handling
    -   Sanitize local spec file paths to reject path traversal sequences (`../`) and absolute paths
    -   Sanitize custom field text values from Compass API to prevent XSS in rendered markdown

## 0.2.0

### Minor Changes

-   a547fd4: feat: map Compass DEPENDS_ON relationships to service markdown
    -   Parse `relationships.DEPENDS_ON` from Compass YAML and resolve referenced services
    -   Add a "Dependencies" section to generated service markdown with links to dependent services
    -   Use a two-pass approach: first collect all services, then resolve dependencies between them
    -   Handle missing dependency targets gracefully (log warning, don't crash)
    -   Services without dependencies show "No known dependencies."

## 0.1.0

### Minor Changes

-   d3f5ee0: Modernize and fix core gaps (Phase 1)
    -   Replace local Service type with SDK's Service type for richer metadata support
    -   Map Compass lifecycle, tier, and labels to EventCatalog badges
    -   Map Compass repository links to service repository URL
    -   Map Compass ownerId to service owners
    -   Support updating existing services with overrideExisting option (defaults to true)
    -   Add Zod validation for generator configuration with clear error messages

-   26590b0: Support all Compass component types (Phase 2)
    -   Remove SERVICE-only restriction — all Compass types (APPLICATION, LIBRARY, CAPABILITY, CLOUD_RESOURCE, DATA_PIPELINE, MACHINE_LEARNING_MODEL, OTHER, UI_ELEMENT, WEBSITE) are now processed as EventCatalog services
    -   Add `typeFilter` option to selectively process only specified component types
    -   Add component type badge showing the Compass type (e.g. APPLICATION, LIBRARY) on each service

## 0.0.9

### Patch Changes

-   e6e0f5c: Phase 1: Update dependencies to latest versions

    Updated dependencies to latest versions (Phase 1 of modernization plan):

    -   @eventcatalog/sdk: ^2.0.0 → ^2.9.0 (9 minor versions, no breaking changes)
    -   @changesets/cli: ^2.28.1 → ^2.29.7
    -   chalk: ^5.4.1 → ^5.6.2

    All tests pass with updated dependencies. See UPDATE_PLAN.md for full modernization roadmap.

## 0.0.8

### Patch Changes

-   3c0a302: Updating depencencies and moving to eventcatalog/sdk 2.0.0

## 0.0.7

### Patch Changes

-   06e4c92: Fix docs and update the dependencies

## 0.0.6

### Patch Changes

-   5600c7a: fix chalk version to 4 to avoid ESM breaking changes

## 0.0.5

### Patch Changes

-   7bfa0ac: update dependencies and adding default PROJECT_DIR when not present

## 0.0.4

### Patch Changes

-   85cf3f5: update dependencies and improve documentation

## 0.0.3

### Patch Changes

-   8694a60: Fixing #9 by moving away from Tiles as they don't support external links on bundle objects

## 0.0.2

### Patch Changes

-   92170b3: Initial BETA release

releases.body:
First tagged release of VoteScot ahead of the 7 May 2026 Scottish Parliament election.

Covers all 73 constituencies with 434 candidates across 7 major parties. Features include the vote compass quiz, interactive constituency map with postcode lookup, polling trends, constituency projections with methodology transparency, candidate profiles with track records, and a side-by-side comparison view.

releases.name: v1.1.0 — Curated Channels
releases.body:
## What's new

- 44 hand-picked channels (UK news, entertainment, movies, music, sports, kids, docs) as the default experience
- Automated channel validation script testing streams from iptv-org playlists
- Weekly GitHub Action keeps validation data fresh
- 1,800+ validated channels available via Find > Validated for discovery
- Split export buttons (All / Favs) to prevent accidental favourites-only export
- Wrapping category filter pills instead of horizontal scroll
- New channels highlight in sidebar when added from Find panel
- Community health files (code of conduct, issue/PR templates)

## Breaking changes

None — the app loads picks.json as the default channel list, falling back to the built-in list if offline.
